evals.prompt: I have a Postgres table called `transactions` with columns: user_id, amount, merchant, country, device_fingerprint, created_at. I want to flag fraudulent transactions in real time. I have 90 days of historical data I can use to build a baseline. Transactions over $5,000 from a new device in a new country at 3am should definitely be critical. Normal everyday purchases should pass through. Can you build a DSPy fraud scorer with severity levels and explanations?
evals.expected_output: A Python script that builds a baseline summary from historical transaction data, defines a DSPy signature with event + baseline_summary inputs and severity (Literal with normal/low/medium/high/critical), explanation, and anomaly_score outputs, uses ChainOfThought, and includes routing logic that maps severity to actions (dismiss, log, alert, block).

assertions.name: uses_dspy_signature
assertions.description: Defines a dspy.Signature class for anomaly scoring

assertions.name: includes_baseline_input
assertions.description: Signature has a baseline_summary input field

assertions.name: uses_literal_severity
assertions.description: Uses Literal type with graduated severity levels (at minimum normal, medium, high, critical)

assertions.name: includes_explanation_output
assertions.description: Signature outputs an explanation field

assertions.name: includes_anomaly_score
assertions.description: Signature outputs a numeric anomaly score (float)

assertions.name: has_routing_logic
assertions.description: Includes severity-to-action routing (different actions for different severity levels)

assertions.name: uses_chain_of_thought
assertions.description: Uses ChainOfThought module for reasoning before scoring

evals.prompt: We run a SaaS app and need to detect account takeovers. The pattern is usually: login from new country, then immediately change password and email, then export data. Individual events look OK but the session pattern is the giveaway. Can you build something that scores a sequence of user session events against that users normal behavior profile?
evals.expected_output: A Python script with a DSPy signature that takes session_events (JSON array of chronological events) and user_baseline as inputs, outputs severity, explanation, and anomaly_score. Should use ChainOfThought and explicitly mention that session-level context matters more than individual events. Should include an example of what a suspicious vs normal session looks like.

assertions.name: uses_session_events_input
assertions.description: Signature takes a session_events input (array or JSON of multiple events)

assertions.name: uses_baseline_input
assertions.description: Signature takes a user_baseline or baseline_summary input

assertions.name: outputs_severity
assertions.description: Signature outputs a severity field with graduated levels

assertions.name: outputs_explanation
assertions.description: Signature outputs an explanation field

assertions.name: explains_session_context
assertions.description: Code or comments explain why session-level batching matters for detecting multi-step attacks

assertions.name: includes_example_session
assertions.description: Includes at least one example of a suspicious session with multiple events

assertions.name: uses_chain_of_thought
assertions.description: Uses ChainOfThought for reasoning

evals.prompt: I have server logs coming in as JSON lines. I want to flag security anomalies — things like SQL injection attempts, scanning for .env files, credential stuffing (many 401s from one IP), and unusual 500 error spikes. Normal traffic is mostly GET requests to /api/v1/* with a 0.3% error rate. I want to process them in 5-minute windows and only alert on medium severity or above.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that processes log entries in batches/windows, defines a DSPy signature with log_batch and baseline_summary inputs and severity + explanation + anomaly_score outputs, includes filtering to only alert on medium/high/critical severity, and optionally lists the specific anomalous log entries identified.

assertions.name: uses_dspy_signature
assertions.description: Defines a dspy.Signature for log anomaly scoring

assertions.name: processes_in_batches
assertions.description: Groups log entries into windows or batches rather than scoring one line at a time

assertions.name: includes_baseline
assertions.description: Uses a baseline_summary input to establish normal log patterns

assertions.name: filters_by_severity
assertions.description: Only generates alerts for medium severity or above, suppresses normal and low

assertions.name: outputs_explanation
assertions.description: Outputs a specific explanation citing paths, IPs, or error patterns from the logs

assertions.name: handles_json_logs
assertions.description: Processes log entries as JSON objects
